content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Eric Lauterer 
city Attorney 
11391 Acacia Parkway 
P.o. Box 3070 
Garden Grove, CA 
92642 
Dear Mr. Lauterer: 
December 15, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 87-317 
You have requested advice concerning interpretation of 
Elections Code section 10211, which governs candidate ballot 
designations. 
The Commission's jurisdiction is limited to 
matters governed by the Political Reform Act (Government Code 
Sections 81000-91015): therefore, we cannot advise you on the 
interpretation of other laws such as Elections Code 
section 10211. 
Your question is more appropriately addressed 
to the Secretary of State's or Attorney General's office. 
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
DMG:KED:plh 
Sincerely, 
Diane M: Griffiths 
General Counsel

By: 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Divsion 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


CITY OF GARDEN GR9;,Y7' CALIFORNIA 
1 1 39 1 A C A C I A PAR K WAY. P O. BOX 3 0 7 O. GAR D 'E Nt IS R 0,..\1 Er .• C A L I FOR N I A 9 2 6 4 2 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Re: 
Request for Opinion on 
Ballot Designations 
-
U 
; 'Jl 
December 4, 1987 
In connection with a recent City of Garden Grove election, my City 
Council has requested that I seek and obtain an opinion as to the follow-
ing subject matter. 
Under the provisions of Election Code Section 10211, is there any 
authorization contained therein which allows any of the following desig-
nations to be used in connection with a candidate running for municipal 
election in a general law city, including that of the office of elected 
Mayor? 
Mayor Pro Tern; Taxfighter; Taxfighter Businessman, or Taxfighter 
Councilman. 
As a matter of background, the City of Garden Grove is a general law 
city composed of five (5) Councilmembers. 
One of the five positions, by 
virtue of a previous election, is the mayoral position and is voted on 
separately. 
The term is set at two (2) years,. while the remaining 
Councilmembers serve four (4) year terms. 
Any interpretation and information you can provide on this subject is 
greatly appreciated. Thank you for your courtesy in this matter. 
~ectfUllY' 
ER;~ 
City Attorney 
EL/pw

sections.question: Under the provisions of Election Code Section 10211, is there any authorization contained therein which allows any of the following designations to be used in connection with a candidate running for municipal election in a general law city, including that of the office of elected Mayor? Mayor Pro Tem; Taxfighter; Taxfighter Businessman, or Taxfighter Councilman.
sections.conclusion: The Commission's jurisdiction is limited to matters governed by the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015); therefore, we cannot advise you on the interpretation of other laws such as Elections Code section 10211. Your question is more appropriately addressed to the Secretary of State's or Attorney General's office.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Elections Code Section 10211 authorizes specific ballot designations (Mayor Pro Tem, Taxfighter, Taxfighter Businessman, or Taxfighter Councilman) for candidates in a general law city municipal election, including the mayoral position.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the question concerns Elections Code Section 10211, which falls outside the Commission's jurisdiction limited to the Political Reform Act. The requester was directed to the Secretary of State's or Attorney General's office for guidance on this matter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter due to lack of FPPC jurisdiction. Both incoming request letter and FPPC response included in document. Response letter dated December 15, 1987; incoming request letter dated December 4, 1987. File No. 87-317.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the provisions of Election Code Section 10211, is there any authorization contained therein which allows any of the following designations to be used in connection with a candidate running for municipal election in a general law city, including that of the office of elected Mayor? Mayor Pro Tem; Taxfighter; Taxfighter Businessman, or Taxfighter Councilman.

CONCLUSION: The Commission's jurisdiction is limited to matters governed by the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015); therefore, we cannot advise you on the interpretation of other laws such as Elections Code section 10211. Your question is more appropriately addressed to the Secretary of State's or Attorney General's office.

embedding.summary: City Attorney Eric Lauterer requested FPPC advice on whether specific ballot designations are authorized under Elections Code Section 10211 for municipal candidates. The FPPC declined jurisdiction and redirected the question to the Secretary of State or Attorney General.
